channel.title: Frontion — Global Geopolitics Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: Trump Orders Navy to Shoot Iranian Boats Laying Mines in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: 🇺🇸 US-Iran War | BBC — President Trump authorized the US Navy to attack any Iranian boats found laying mines in the Strait of Hormuz, stating forces can "shoot and kill" such vessels. The order comes amid continued tensions despite a ceasefire extension.

item.title: Trump Tells BBC King Charles Visit Could Help Repair UK-US Relations
item.description: 🇬🇧 Diplomacy | BBC — In a phone interview with the BBC's North America editor, President Trump discussed next week's royal visit and his relationship with the UK Prime Minister, suggesting the visit could "absolutely" help repair strained relations.

item.title: More Than 500 Killed in Tanzania Election Violence, Inquiry Finds
item.description: 🇹🇿 Africa | BBC — An official inquiry found over 500 people were killed in Tanzania election violence. Opposition parties accused security forces but the report does not specify who was responsible.

item.title: ICC Confirms Crimes Against Humanity Trial for Ex-Philippine Leader Duterte
item.description: 🇵🇭 Asia-Pacific | Al Jazeera — ICC judges ruled there are substantial grounds to believe former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte guided an anti-drugs crackdown that killed thousands, clearing the way for a crimes against humanity trial.

item.title: US Senate Passes Billion Funding Plan for ICE and Border Patrol
item.description: 🇺🇸 US Politics | Al Jazeera — Republicans passed the first step of a major immigration enforcement funding package after a marathon vote-a-rama, but face a long road to break the impasse over funding Trump's border policies.

item.title: US Navy Secretary John Phelan leaving post immediately amid shipbuilding tensions
item.description: 🇺🇸⚓ US Politics | AP News — Pentagon says Navy Secretary Phelan is departing "effective immediately," the latest in a series of top defense leadership exits. Reports cite tensions over US shipbuilding and naval readiness.

item.title: More than 500 people killed in Tanzania election violence, inquiry finds
item.description: 🇹🇿⚖️ Africa | BBC — An official inquiry reports over 500 deaths in Tanzania election violence. Opposition parties accused security forces, but the report does not specify who was responsible for the killings.

item.title: Trump reclassifies medical marijuana as less-dangerous drug in historic shift
item.description: 🇺🇸💊 US Politics | AP News — The Trump administration has moved state-licensed medical marijuana into a lower drug category, a historic policy shift aimed at increasing research and access.

item.title: Two trains collide head-on in Denmark, five critically injured
item.description: 🇩🇰🚂 Europe | BBC — A head-on train collision north of Copenhagen has left five people critically injured. The accident occurred at a level crossing during early Thursday hours.

item.title: Condom prices could rise 30% due to Iran war, says world top producer
item.description: 💰⚔️ Economy | The Guardian — Karex, the world largest condom producer, warns prices could increase 30% due to supply chain disruptions from the Iran conflict, affecting global healthcare supplies.

item.title: Israeli soldiers testify to systematic looting in Lebanon with commanders knowledge
item.description: 🇮🇱 Middle East | Haaretz — Haaretz reports Israeli soldiers have testified to widespread looting of homes in southern Lebanon, with claims that commanders are aware but taking no action. The report adds to growing scrutiny of Israeli military conduct during the conflict.

item.title: Germany unveils strategy to become Europes strongest military by 2039
item.description: 🇩🇪 Europe | Defense News — Defense News reports Germany has unveiled an ambitious plan to build Europes most powerful military within 15 years, citing the Russian threat and the need for European self-reliance amid US focus on Iran.

item.title: Ukraine signs drone defense deal with Saudi Arabia Qatar and UAE
item.description: 🇺🇦 Ukraine-Russia War | TRT World — President Zelensky announced agreements with Gulf states to supply interceptor drones capable of destroying Iranian Shahed drones at a fraction of their cost. The deal strengthens Ukraines air defense capabilities.

item.title: UK health data of 500000 people listed for sale in China
item.description: 🇬🇧 Cyber | Straits Times — The Straits Times reports UK health records of half a million people have been discovered for sale in China, prompting outrage and concerns about data security in an already strained bilateral relationship.

item.title: Russia and North Korea open new road bridge linking the two countries
item.description: 🇷🇺 Diplomacy | The Independent — A ground-breaking ceremony was held for a new road bridge spanning the Tumen River that will directly connect North Korea to Russia, deepening the strategic partnership between Moscow and Pyongyang.

item.title: US forces board Iranian oil tanker, Trump orders navy to shoot boats laying mines in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: 🇺🇸🇮🇷⚔️ US-Iran War | BBC — The US military intercepted an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Gulf as part of its naval blockade. Trump declared the US has "total control" over the Strait of Hormuz and authorized lethal force against any boats laying mines.

item.title: Israel kills Lebanese journalist Amal Khalil, drawing international condemnation
item.description: 🇱🇧📰💔 Middle East | The Guardian — Lebanese PM called the attack a "war crime" after journalist Amal Khalil was killed in what officials described as a "double-tap" strike. Her death has prompted fresh claims about Israel targeting media workers.

item.title: EU formally approves €90 billion Ukraine loan and 20th Russia sanctions package
item.description: 🇪🇺🇺🇦💰 Ukraine-Russia War | The Guardian — The European Union gave final approval to a massive €90 billion loan package for Ukraine, heavily weighted toward military spending, reflecting the view that peace in Ukraine remains distant.

item.title: Millions in India stripped of vote before critical West Bengal state election
item.description: 🇮🇳🗳️ South Asia | The Guardian — Experts say Muslims and other minorities have been disproportionately deleted from electoral rolls ahead of the West Bengal elections, as the government seeks to "purify" voter lists.

item.title: About 250 Rohingya refugees missing after boat capsizes in Andaman Sea
item.description: 🚨🌊 South Asia | The Guardian — A boat carrying Rohingya refugees capsized in the Andaman Sea, leaving approximately 250 people missing in one of the deadliest maritime incidents involving the persecuted minority in recent months.

item.title: US Orders Military to Shoot Iranian Mine-Laying Boats in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: 🇺🇸 US-Iran War | AP News — President Trump authorized the US Navy to "shoot and kill" any Iranian boats deploying mines to choke traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, escalating the US-Iran standoff. The order comes as Iran attacked three cargo ships and captured two in the strait following Trump's ceasefire extension announcement.

item.title: ICC Confirms Crimes Against Humanity Trial for Former Philippine President Duterte
item.description: 🇵🇭 Asia-Pacific | Al Jazeera — The International Criminal Court confirmed all charges against Rodrigo Duterte for his "war on drugs" that killed thousands, committing the 81-year-old former president to trial. Judges said there were substantial grounds to believe he played a key role in 76 murders.

item.title: Navy Secretary John Phelan Abruptly Departs Pentagon
item.description: 🇺🇸 US Politics | AP News — The Pentagon announced Navy Secretary John Phelan is leaving his position with no reason given, the first head of a military service to depart during Trump's second term. The departure comes amid the Navy's blockade of Iranian ports and targeting of Iranian-linked ships worldwide.

item.title: US Senate Advances $70 Billion Funding for ICE and Border Patrol
item.description: 🇺🇸 US Politics | Al Jazeera — The Senate voted 50-48 early Thursday to advance funding for Immigration and Customs Enforcement and Border Patrol, taking the first steps toward reopening the Department of Homeland Security. The department has been partially shut down since mid-February.

item.title: More Than 500 Killed in Tanzania Election Violence, Inquiry Finds
item.description: 🇹🇿 Africa | BBC — A commission of inquiry reported that 518 people died from "unnatural causes" following protests after Tanzania's disputed election where President Samia Suluhu Hassan won with 98% of the vote. Opposition parties accused security forces of a brutal crackdown.
